mission.title: Install and Configure IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS)
mission.description: IBM Cloud Kubernetes Service (IKS) is a managed Kubernetes offering that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on IBM Cloud. It is ideal for users looking for a robust, scalable solution without the overhead of managing the underlying infrastructure. Key trade-offs include reliance on IBM's cloud services and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the IBM Cloud CLI
steps.description:
The IBM Cloud CLI is required to interact with the IBM Cloud services, including creating and managing Kubernetes clusters.

```bash

curl -sL https://ibm.biz/idt-installer | bash

```

> This command installs the IBM Cloud CLI.

steps.title: Log in to IBM Cloud
steps.description:
Authenticate your IBM Cloud account to access your resources.

```bash

ibmcloud login

```

> Follow the prompts to enter your IBM Cloud credentials.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-create --name my-cluster --zone us-south-1 --kube-version 1.30 --num-nodes 3

```

> Adjust the zone and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster

```

> This command sets the kubeconfig context for your cluster.

steps.title: Verify cluster nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-installation configuration
steps.description:
Consider enabling autoscaling, monitoring, and setting up RBAC for security.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --enable-autoscale

ibmcloud ks cluster-monitoring-enable --cluster my-cluster

```

> Enables autoscaling for your cluster.
> Enables monitoring for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes cluster
uninstall.description:
This will remove the cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this action cannot be undone and may leave orphaned resources.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-delete --cluster my-cluster --force

```

> Use --force to skip confirmation.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
Check for and manually delete any remaining resources such as load balancers or persistent volumes.

```bash

ibmcloud resource service-instance-list

```

> List all resources to identify any orphaned ones.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your workloads and configurations. After the upgrade, verify the cluster is functioning correctly.

```bash

ibmcloud ks cluster-update --cluster my-cluster --kube-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> This command upgrades your cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' state after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your IBM Cloud CLI session has expired or you are not logged in.

**Fix:**
Re-run 'ibmcloud login' to authenticate again.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the cluster is not properly configured or if the kubeconfig context is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Run 'ibmcloud ks cluster-config --cluster my-cluster' to set the correct context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the requested resources exceed the available quota in your IBM Cloud account.

**Fix:**
Check your resource quotas and adjust your cluster specifications accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Cluster not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified cluster name does not exist in your account.

**Fix:**
Verify the cluster name with 'ibmcloud ks clusters' and ensure you are using the correct name.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 features, including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecation of certain legacy APIs; refer to the IBM Cloud documentation for details.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure to review the deprecated APIs and adjust your workloads accordingly.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the IBM Cloud CLI installed and configured.
